content.question: How much of the documented concentration of disadvantaged students in specific schools (particularly Northeastern villages and segregated 'ghetto schools') is driven by residential demographic sorting versus free school-choice induced mobility and white flight?
content.reduction_path: Decompose segregation indices into residential-catchment component and inter-district mobility component using administrative data on pupil residence, school assignment, and actual enrollment; compare segregation levels in regions with stronger versus weaker school-choice enforcement; track cohorts of families across decades to measure switching patterns
